raw_consumer_statement:
XXXX XXXX # XXXX Ordered a jacket online on XX/XX/XXXX. Was supposedly delivered on XX/XX/XXXX via XXXX ( originally shipped via FedEx ). Never received item. Reached out to XXXX ( via phone ) and was told to wait XXXX days, as XXXX marking an item " delivered '' may simply mean that it was on the truck for delivery. XXXX days went by and I reached out to XXXX again XXXX via phone ) and was told I needed to wait 5 days to file a claim. Filed a claim online for " Item Not Received. '' XXXX business days go by and it was denied. I called XXXX again ( via phone ) to appeal the claim. Another XXXX business days go by and I got an email that it was denied. 

I then opened a dispute with American Express. This dispute was opened/closed XXXX separate times. The first time, it was simply closed. The second and third times, American Express sided with XXXX and provided documentation with XXXX things ( XXXX ) proof that I initiated the order ( not disputed ) ; XXXX ) XXXX  tracking showing that the item was delivered ( no signature, photo, or proof of delivery ) ; 3 ) return policy stating that since 30 days had elapsed, XXXX would no longer accept a return ( I can't return an item I never received ) ). Since XXXX provided those documents, AmEx refuses to honor the dispute. I've even spent days and weeks on the phone with AmEx 3-way calling XXXX to try to get XXXX to share a proof of delivery or proof that they had opened a claim with XXXX. I never got proof that they opened any inquiry with XXXX. XXXX will not open a claim for me, as I am not the shipper. 

At various times, American Express disputes team told me : - That I could file a " XXXXhase Protection '' claim. Their purchase protection terms state that items in the possession of a " common carrier '' are not covered. As I never took delivery of the item, I could not say it was stolen from me.

- American Express requested multiple times via 3-way calls with XXXX that they provide proof of delivery ( signature or photo ) or proof that XXXX initiated a claim with XXXX. XXXX never provided either. 
- American Express told me that they would write off the charge. Then at another point they told me they would offer me half of the charge as a gesture ~ {$380.00}. Ended up offering me nothing. 

I am at a complete loss at this point. This was an expensive item that I bought myself for my birthday. I don't know what other recourse I have. I thought XXXX would have opened an investigation with FedEx and replaced the item. I thought AmEx would have my back once I filed a dispute. Please help.
